channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh Faces Pressure to Balance White House Rate-Cut Demands Against Strong Economic Data
item.description: A Wall Street Journal report highlighted growing tensions between the White House and newly appointed Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh over the future direction of U.S. interest rates. President Dona...

item.title: China Expands Humanoid Robot Production Despite Uncertain Commercial Demand
item.description: China is rapidly increasing its production of humanoid robots and has emerged as the dominant force in the global market, benefiting from extensive manufacturing capabilities, government support and a...

item.title: Woodkid Reimagines 'Leave Her Johnny' for Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Remake Launching July 9
item.description: Ubisoft has revealed that the upcoming remake of Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag will feature a newly recorded version of the popular sea shanty 'Leave Her Johnny', performed by French musician Woodkid. ...

item.title: Reid Hoffman steps down from Microsoft board to focus on AI drug discovery startup Manus
item.description: Reid Hoffman, the LinkedIn co-founder and long-time Microsoft board member, is stepping down from Microsoft’s board after a decade of service to dedicate more time to his AI-focused startup Manus. Hof...

item.title: BOC Says No Confirmed Bag Tag Switching Cases in Philippine Airports; P13.3 Million Worth of Kush Seized
item.description: The Bureau of Customs (BOC) clarified that there have been no confirmed incidents of baggage tag switching at any Philippine airport in recent months, addressing concerns raised by reports circulating...

item.title: DOJ official Todd Blanche says he is creating internal safeguards to limit future prosecutions of Donald Trump
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche has stated that he is putting in place what he described as “roadblocks” within the Department of Justice aimed at preventing potential future prosecutions of...

item.title: Political Analyst Describes How Senate Republicans Block Some Trump Priorities by Avoiding Votes
item.description: A Raw Story article highlights comments made during a New York Times discussion about how Senate Republicans can prevent certain priorities of President Donald Trump from advancing without directly op...

item.title: Jon Snow, Longtime Channel 4 News Anchor, Reveals Alzheimer's Diagnosis
item.description: Jon Snow, the veteran British journalist who served as the lead presenter of Channel 4 News for over three decades, has publicly disclosed his diagnosis with Alzheimer's disease. At 78 years old, Snow...

item.title: Iowa Democratic Candidate Rob Sand Warns of State Budget Deficit Risks in Gubernatorial Campaign
item.description: In a speech at the Iowa Association of Business and Industry conference, Democratic gubernatorial candidate and current State Auditor Rob Sand emphasized bipartisanship as he campaigns against Republi...

item.title: Kodachrome Photographs Document Daily Life and Landmarks in San Francisco During 1965
item.description: This article presents a collection of 30 Kodachrome color slides capturing scenes from San Francisco throughout 1965, offering a vivid visual record of the city during a period of cultural transition....

item.title: AFP and US Indo-Pacific Command hold joint disaster response exercise under MPAT program
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) and the United States Indo-Pacific Command have started a joint training exercise focused on disaster preparedness and response, rather than combat operations...

item.title: White House Denies Report Claiming Susie Wiles Plans to Leave Chief of Staff Role After Midterms
item.description: The article reports on a dispute between the White House and media reports regarding the future of White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles. According to a report published by the Daily Mail, Wiles was ...

item.title: Supreme Court Upholds FCC Authority to Enforce Telecom Data Privacy Rules in Case Involving Verizon and AT&T
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled 8-1 in favor of the Trump administration, preserving the Federal Communications Commission’s (FCC) authority to enforce federal data privacy regulations against telecommun...

item.title: Stefan Falke Uses Handheld Mirrors to Reimagine New York Street Photography
item.description: LensCulture highlights the work of photographer Stefan Falke, whose project 'Reflecting New York' presents a distinctive approach to street photography. By using a handheld mirror in various locations...

item.title: Russian Survey Ship Akademik Alexander Karpinsky Shows Signs of AIS Spoofing Amid Antarctic Prospecting Allegations
item.description: A Russian seismic survey vessel, the Akademik Alexander Karpinsky, owned by state-owned Rosgeo, has been at the center of a multi-year investigation by Daily Maverick regarding potential violations of...

item.title: Trump Criticizes Sen. Thom Tillis After Conditions Placed on Todd Blanche Confirmation
item.description: President Donald Trump sharply criticized Senator Thom Tillis of North Carolina after the Republican senator indicated that he would not support the confirmation of Todd Blanche as attorney general un...

item.title: Nicola Walker and Jemaine Clement discuss Alice and Steve, a comedy-drama exploring friendship, betrayal and family conflict
item.description: The article examines the new Disney+ series Alice and Steve, starring Nicola Walker and Jemaine Clement and created by Sophie Goodhart. The programme centres on the long-standing friendship between Al...

item.title: Texas Governor Declares Statewide Disaster Due to New World Screwworm Detection in Livestock
item.description: Texas Governor Greg Abbott has declared a state of disaster across all 254 counties in response to the detection of the New World Screwworm, a parasitic fly whose larvae feed on the living tissue of w...

item.title: Sen. Ron Johnson Questions Ebola Vaccine Preparedness During Interview on Public Health Risks
item.description: A news report focuses on comments made by U.S. Senator Ron Johnson of Wisconsin during an interview on LindellTV, where he expressed skepticism about the need for Ebola vaccines and criticized what he...

item.title: Nearly 60 Idaho Residents Sickened by Raw Milk in Recent Outbreak
item.description: Idaho health officials are investigating an outbreak of illnesses linked to raw milk consumption that has affected nearly 60 people across the state in the past two weeks. The cases began appearing ar...
